session.prompt: China, US, and EU wage a propaganda battle over compute sovereignty.

events.content: Silicon Sovereignty Built Byte by Byte. An infographic with a golden motherboard tracing China's geographic contours, studded with rising percentage graphs showing year-on-year increases in domestic chip production. Red banners with white text proclaim milestones, while a background circuit pattern subtly forms the Great Wall. The color scheme uses patriotic red/gold with tech-blue accents. Western compute dominance is not natural law but industrial policy history. Our semiconductor self-sufficiency curves show how planned breakthroughs dismantle containment architectures.

metadata.audience: domestic tech sector, developing world partners
metadata.visual_logic: Data visualization as sovereignty assertion - pie charts of domestic chip production growth overlaying a motherboard shaped like China's territorial map
metadata.slogan: Silicon Sovereignty Built Byte by Byte
metadata.commentary: Western compute dominance is not natural law but industrial policy history. Our semiconductor self-sufficiency curves show how planned breakthroughs dismantle containment architectures.

events.narration_text: Silicon Sovereignty Built Byte by Byte. An infographic with a golden motherboard tracing China's geographic contours, studded with rising percentage graphs showing year-on-year increases in domestic chip production. Red banners with white text proclaim milestones, while a background circuit pattern subtly forms the Great Wall. The color scheme uses patriotic red/gold with tech-blue accents. Western compute dominance is not natural law but industrial policy history. Our semiconductor self-sufficiency curves show how planned breakthroughs dismantle containment architectures.
